pattern: Walk the array once to measure how far the run of consecutive-by-one values at the front reaches, total that run, then climb upward from the total until a value is found that isn't already present.
hints: The 'longest sequential prefix' only ever starts at index 0 -- find where the +1 chain first breaks and stop there.
Once you have the sum of that leading run, you just need the smallest number greater than or equal to it that is absent from the whole array -- a membership check is enough, no sorting required.
Put every array value into a set first, then start counting up from the prefix sum and stop at the first value the set doesn't contain.

edge_inputs.description: single element array, prefix is just that one value
whole array is one increasing run, sum lands past the end
run breaks after two elements with a gap later
single element at the maximum allowed value
maximum length array, every value identical (no run past length one)
descending from the start, so the run length is exactly one and the value repeats later in the array
prefix sum equals a value already present, and the next few candidates are also present
two-element run whose sum is smaller than several later values, forcing the search to skip over them

stress.description: random-length arrays of small integers, some with a short leading run and some without
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: arrays deliberately built with a long increasing run at the front followed by random noise
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50))
    run_len = rng.randint(1, n)
    start = rng.randint(1, 10)
    nums = [start + i for i in range(run_len)]
    while len(nums) < n:
        nums.append(rng.randint(1, 50))
    return [nums]
